comment: Per-embodiment rule for converting action_native -> action_canonical_ee[7]. Canonical EE frame: [lin_vel_xyz (m/s), ang_vel_xyz (rad/s), gripper (0=open, 1=closed)]. Each rule has 'kind' and per-kind parameters; see prep.stage_3_canonical for the implementations.

agibot_g1.comment: AgiBot G1 native action is a 24-D bimanual arm+hand command. We canonicalize using the right-arm EE-pose stream from the parquet `observation.state` columns and finite-difference it. Stage_3 reads the pre-computed EE-frame velocities supplied by the converter (which already returns action_canonical_ee[T, 7]) and only re-validates here. The 'indices' below define the right-arm EE columns inside action_native if a downstream caller wants to redo the diff.

rh20t_franka.comment: RH20T's action is a 6-DoF EE pose (xyz + axis-angle) plus gripper. We finite-difference consecutive poses to get EE velocity, leaving gripper as-is.

robomind_tien_kung.comment: RoboMIND Tien Kung native action is bimanual joint positions (7+7). We expect the converter to have already produced action_canonical_ee from the per-frame EE pose streams (TF tree from urdf). Stage_3 only re-validates.

bridge_widowx.comment: BridgeData V2 native action is already 6-DoF delta-pose + gripper at 5 Hz. We pass through and treat the deltas as approximate velocities (the dt is the same across all frames).
